edges.description: Loss of ALG12 function impairs N-glycan precursor formation and transfer to proteins.
Impaired ALG12 activity shifts serum N-glycan structures toward truncated and abnormal high-mannose or hybrid species.
Abnormal N-glycan precursor formation affects developmentally important glycoproteins and is associated with neurologic and brain manifestations.
The ALG12-linked glycosylation defect is associated with impaired growth and feeding failure in reported patients.
Multisystem ALG12-CDG presentations include craniofacial dysmorphism and male genital hypoplasia.
Severe prenatal or neonatal ALG12-CDG presentations can include renal and skeletal developmental abnormalities.
An unusually mild adult presentation included congenital cardiac and spinal structural findings, with mechanism and modifier contribution still uncertain.
Abnormal IgG N-glycosylation and immune-system involvement contribute to hypogammaglobulinemia.
Immunoglobulin deficiency contributes to recurrent infections.
Hypoglycosylation affects plasma proteins involved in coagulation.
Developmental delay is part of the reported ALG12-CDG neurologic spectrum.
Intellectual disability is part of the reported ALG12-CDG neurologic spectrum.
Hypotonia is part of the reported ALG12-CDG neurologic spectrum.
Failure to thrive is a reported growth manifestation of ALG12-CDG.
Short stature is a reported growth manifestation of ALG12-CDG.
Dysmorphic features include craniofacial involvement.
Male genital hypoplasia is a genitourinary developmental manifestation.
Skeletal dysplasia-like abnormalities are part of the severe neonatal branch.
Multicystic kidneys expand the renal phenotype of severe ALG12-CDG.
Ventricular septal defect was reported in the mild adult ALG12-CDG case.
Severe scoliosis was reported in the mild adult ALG12-CDG case.
A type I transferrin hypoglycosylation pattern reports impaired N-glycosylation-site occupancy downstream of the ALG12 precursor defect.
Accumulation of GlcNAc2Man5-7 with reduced GlcNAc2Man8-9 reports impaired ALG12 enzymatic activity.
Hypoglycosylated coagulation proteins report systemic plasma-protein hypoglycosylation caused by the ALG12-CDG glycosylation defect.

evidence.reference_title: A novel homozygous mutation in the human ALG12 gene results in an aberrant profile of oligomannose N-glycans in patient's serum.
evidence.snippet: Genetic analysis of the coding regions of the ALG12 gene of the patient revealed a novel homozygous substitution mutation c.1439T>C p.(Leu480Pro) within Exon 10.

evidence.reference_title: Expanded prenatal phenotype of ALG12-associated congenital disorder of glycosylation including bilateral multicystic kidneys.
evidence.snippet: We report on a case expanding the phenotype of ALG12-CDG to include bilateral, multicystic kidneys in a neonatal demise identified with homozygous pathogenic variants in the ALG12 gene at c.1001del (p.N334Tfs*15) through clinical trio exome sequencing.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12 / ALG12-congenital disorder of glycosylation (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: In summary, there is definitive evidence to support the relationship between ALG12 and autosomal recessive ALG12 -congenital disorder of glycosylation.

nodes.description: Pathogenic ALG12 variants impair the alpha-mannosyltransferase step needed for dolichol-linked oligosaccharide biosynthesis and N-glycan precursor formation.

evidence.reference_title: A novel homozygous mutation in the human ALG12 gene results in an aberrant profile of oligomannose N-glycans in patient's serum.
evidence.snippet: Congenital disorder of glycosylation type Ig (ALG12-CDG) is a rare inherited metabolic disease caused by a defect in alpha-mannosyltransferase 8, encoded by the ALG12 gene (22q13.33).

evidence.reference_title: ALG12 / ALG12-congenital disorder of glycosylation (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: The mechanism of pathogenicity is known to be loss-of-function.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12 mannosyltransferase defect in congenital disorder of glycosylation type lg.
evidence.snippet: In the patient's fibroblasts, the biosynthetic intermediate GlcNAc(2)Man(7) oligosaccharide was detected both on the lipid carrier dolichyl pyrophosphate and on newly synthesized glycoproteins, thus pointing to a defect in the dolichyl pyrophosphate-GlcNAc(2)Man(7)-dependent ALG12 alpha1,6 mannosyltransferase.

evidence.reference_title: A novel homozygous mutation in the human ALG12 gene results in an aberrant profile of oligomannose N-glycans in patient's serum.
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, analysis of neutral serum N-glycans by mass spectrometry revealed the accumulation of GlcNAc2Man5-7 and decreased levels of GlcNAc2Man8-9, which indicated impaired ALG12 enzymatic activity.

readouts.interpretation: Accumulation of GlcNAc2Man5-7 with reduced GlcNAc2Man8-9 reports impaired ALG12 enzymatic activity.

nodes.description: Abnormal N-glycosylation of serum and IgG proteins contributes to multisystem manifestations, including immune dysfunction and recurrent infections.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12-CDG: novel glycophenotype insights endorse the molecular defect.
evidence.snippet: ALG12-CDG is a severe multisystem disease associated with low to deficient serum immunoglobulins and recurrent infections.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12-CDG: novel glycophenotype insights endorse the molecular defect.
evidence.snippet: As a whole, ALG12-CDG behaves as a dual CDG (CDG-I and II defects) and it is associated with distinct, abnormal glycosylation of total serum and IgG N-glycans.

nodes.description: ALG12-CDG can involve coagulation factor abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Expanded prenatal phenotype of ALG12-associated congenital disorder of glycosylation including bilateral multicystic kidneys.
evidence.snippet: Currently reported features of ALG12-CDG include: developmental delay, hypotonia, failure to thrive and/or short stature, brain anomalies, recurrent infections, hypogammaglobulinemia, coagulation abnormalities, and genitourinary abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12-CDG: An unusual patient without intellectual disability and facial dysmorphism, and with a novel variant.
evidence.snippet: Increased levels of hypoglycosylated forms of F XI (also with significant deficiency) and transferrin were also detected.

nodes.description: A mild adult ALG12-CDG presentation included ventricular septal defect and severe scoliosis; causal directness and possible modifier contributions are unresolved.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12-CDG: An unusual patient without intellectual disability and facial dysmorphism, and with a novel variant.
evidence.snippet: We here present an intriguing patient with an exceptional phenotype: 25-year-old women with a ventricular septal defect and severe idiopathic scoliosis but no facial dysmorphism, who dances as a professional, and has a University degree.

nodes.description: Hypogammaglobulinemia or low serum immunoglobulins can occur.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12-CDG: novel glycophenotype insights endorse the molecular defect.
evidence.snippet: ALG12-CDG is a severe multisystem disease associated with low to deficient serum immunoglobulins and recurrent infections.

nodes.description: ALG12-CDG can involve craniofacial dysmorphism and male genitourinary developmental abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Complex phenotypes in ALG12-congenital disorder of glycosylation (ALG12-CDG): Case series and review of the literature.
evidence.snippet: ALG12-congenital disorder of glycosylation (ALG12-CDG) is a rare disorder caused by a deficiency of dolichol-P-mannose:Man7GlcNAc2-PP-dolichyl-α-6-mannosyltransferase which presents with intellectual disability, hypotonia, dysmorphic features, low IgG levels with recurrent infections, male genital hypoplasia, and coagulation abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12 / ALG12-congenital disorder of glycosylation (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: Common phenotypes include intellectual disability, hypotonia, dysmorphic features, coagulation abnormalities and genitourinary abnormalities.

nodes.description: Dysmorphic facial features are a common craniofacial manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Complex phenotypes in ALG12-congenital disorder of glycosylation (ALG12-CDG): Case series and review of the literature.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12 / ALG12-congenital disorder of glycosylation (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: Common phenotypes include intellectual disability, hypotonia, dysmorphic features, coagulation abnormalities and genitourinary abnormalities.

nodes.description: Feeding and growth impairment can present as failure to thrive.

nodes.description: Generalized hypotonia is a recurrent neurologic manifestation.

nodes.description: ALG12-CDG can impair somatic growth and feeding, presenting as failure to thrive, growth retardation, anorexia, or short stature.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12 mannosyltransferase defect in congenital disorder of glycosylation type lg.
evidence.snippet: The severe disease was identified in a child presenting with psychomotor retardation, hypotonia, growth retardation, dysmorphic features and anorexia.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12-CDG: An unusual patient without intellectual disability and facial dysmorphism, and with a novel variant.
evidence.snippet: Congenital disorder of glycosylation diagnosis started through the identification of antithrombin deficiency without SERPINC1 defect and the detection of hypoglycosylated forms.

readouts.interpretation: Hypoglycosylated coagulation proteins report systemic plasma-protein hypoglycosylation caused by the ALG12-CDG glycosylation defect.

nodes.description: Male genital hypoplasia is part of the reported ALG12-CDG genitourinary spectrum.

evidence.snippet: Common phenotypes include intellectual disability, hypotonia, dysmorphic features, coagulation abnormalities and genitourinary abnormalities.

nodes.description: Intravenous or subcutaneous immunoglobulin replacement is a disease-specific supportive consideration for ALG12-CDG patients with low immunoglobulins and recurrent infections. Validator-cache evidence supports the antibody-deficiency indication, while direct published treatment-use details are limited.

evidence.reference_title: Expanded prenatal phenotype of ALG12-associated congenital disorder of glycosylation including bilateral multicystic kidneys.
evidence.snippet: We report on a case expanding the phenotype of ALG12-CDG to include bilateral, multicystic kidneys in a neonatal demise identified with homozygous pathogenic variants in the ALG12 gene at c.1001del (p.N334Tfs*15) through clinical trio exome sequencing.

nodes.description: Impaired N-glycan precursor formation leads to hypoglycosylation of transferrin and other plasma proteins, consistent with a type I CDG biochemical pattern.

evidence.reference_title: A novel homozygous mutation in the human ALG12 gene results in an aberrant profile of oligomannose N-glycans in patient's serum.
evidence.snippet: Due to a newborn Slovak patient's clinical and biochemical abnormalities, the isoelectric focusing of transferrin was performed with observed significant hypoglycosylation typical of CDG I.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12-CDG: novel glycophenotype insights endorse the molecular defect.
evidence.snippet: Intact serum transferrin showed, as expected for a CDG type I defect, underoccupancy of N-glycosylation sites.

nodes.description: ALG12-CDG neurodevelopmental involvement includes developmental delay, intellectual disability, hypotonia, and reported brain anomalies.

evidence.reference_title: Expanded prenatal phenotype of ALG12-associated congenital disorder of glycosylation including bilateral multicystic kidneys.

nodes.description: Recurrent respiratory infections reflect immune involvement.

nodes.description: Severe ALG12-CDG can involve prenatal renal malformation and skeletal-dysplasia-like abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Expanded prenatal phenotype of ALG12-associated congenital disorder of glycosylation including bilateral multicystic kidneys.
evidence.snippet: In addition, skeletal abnormalities resembling a skeletal dysplasia including shortened long bones and talipes equinovarus have been seen in more severe neonatal presentation of this disorder.

nodes.description: Scoliosis has been reported in a mild adult presentation.

evidence.reference_title: ALG12-CDG: An unusual patient without intellectual disability and facial dysmorphism, and with a novel variant.
evidence.snippet: We here present an intriguing patient with an exceptional phenotype: 25-year-old women with a ventricular septal defect and severe idiopathic scoliosis but no facial dysmorphism, who dances as a professional, and has a University degree.

evidence.reference_title: Expanded prenatal phenotype of ALG12-associated congenital disorder of glycosylation including bilateral multicystic kidneys.
evidence.snippet: In addition, skeletal abnormalities resembling a skeletal dysplasia including shortened long bones and talipes equinovarus have been seen in more severe neonatal presentation of this disorder.

nodes.description: No ALG12-CDG-specific disease-modifying therapy is established in the fetched evidence; care is supportive and directed to infections, nutrition/growth, neurodevelopmental needs, coagulation abnormalities, and organ-specific complications.

evidence.reference_title: Congenital disorders of glycosylation: narration of a story through its patents.
evidence.snippet: Since the first clinical report in 1980 of PMM2-CDG, the most common CDG worldwide, research made great strides, but nearly all of them are still missing a cure.
